---
title: "AI 4 Coding"
subtitle: "Day 1: Course Overview"
author:
  - name: Eduard Bukin
    orcid: 0000-0002-1003-1260
    github: ebukin
    email: ebukin@worldbank.org
    affiliations: Distributional Impact of Policies. Fiscal Policy and Growth Department
date: "April 20, 2024"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [simple, custom.scss]
    include-after-body:
      - text: |
          <script>
          (function() {
            function toggleFooter() {
              var f = document.querySelector(".reveal > .footer");
              if (!f || typeof Reveal === "undefined") return;
              var i = Reveal.getIndices();
              f.style.display = (i.h === 0 && i.v === 0) ? "none" : "block";
            }
            if (typeof Reveal !== "undefined") {
              Reveal.on("slidechanged", toggleFooter);
              Reveal.on("ready", toggleFooter);
              if (Reveal.isReady()) toggleFooter();
            }
          })();
          </script>
    code-copy: false
    margin: 0.10
    html-math-method: mathjax
    slide-number: c/t
    background-transition: zoom
    preload-iframes: true
    scrollable: true
    transition: fade
    preview-links: auto
    logo: ../../assets/img/WBG-Prosperity Horizontal-Black.svg
    footer: '[worldbank.github.io/ai4coding](https://worldbank.github.io/ai4coding) © 2026 World Bank.'
    resources:
        - ../../assets/img/WBG-Prosperity Horizontal-Black.svg
---


## Motivation

::::::::: {.small}
:::::: columns
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Chat-Based AI tools** [Claude](https://www.anthropic.com/claude) \| [ChatGPT](https://chatgpt.com/) \| [Gemini](https://search.google/ai-in-search/) \| [MAI](https://ai.worldbank.org/chat/mai)

- use familiar technologies: 🌐 Web-Browser, 💬 Chat, 📝 Stata editor, [**📋 copy-paste**]{.warning}
- improve our workflow.

[**Is this the most effective way to use AI in coding❓**]{.fg}
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
There are many [**Integrated** Development Environments (IDEs)]{.highlight} for data science with AI integrated into your coding workflow.
Introducing [**Positron**]{.bg} with [**Github Copilot**]{.fg} is the goal of this course.
:::
::::::

[See also: previous seminar on [AI-Assisted Coding in Stata and R](https://worldbank.github.io/geoPovLearn/seminars/seminar-2026-02/overview.html) on [GeoPovlearn](https://worldbank.github.io/geoPovLearn).]{.center .alert}
:::::::::

## Course Overview {.center .small-slide}

[**Goal:** Equip economists with AI-skills for coding.]{.alert}

[2 × 3-hour sessions]{.pill} [Self-study in between]{.pill} [GitHub Copilot · Positron IDE]{.pill} [Stata & modern stat languages]{.pill}

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="30%" .obj-card .card-understand}
#### Understand

- Core LLM concepts: tokens, prompts, context windows, agents
- How these affect AI-assisted coding
- Strengths and limitations for analytical workflows
:::

::: {.column width="5%" }
:::

::: {.column width="30%" .obj-card .card-apply}
#### Practice

- Use AI to understand, revise and reuse code
- Plan execution and write prompts for multi-step tasks
- Leverage `#tools`, `skills` and `/agents`
- Supervise and course-correct AI-generated code
:::

::: {.column width="5%" }
:::

::: {.column width="30%" .obj-card .card-control}
#### Take Control

- Data-security practices and guardrailing AI.
- Responsible-use guidelines for AI-assisted
:::

::::


## Course Structure {.small-slide}

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="30%" .phase-card .phase-day1}
#### Day 1
**Basics**

- Software setup & walk-through
- Key concepts: IDE, assistants, context, tokens, LLM, and how it works
- Examples: data preparation and cleaning, revising and reusing old code
- Common techniques: managing AI's context and securing your data
:::

::: {.column width="5%"}
:::

::: {.column width="30%" .phase-card .phase-selfstudy}
#### Self-Study
**The most important part**

- Practice AI use with your own code
- Structured case studies to guide you
- Come back with questions & observations
:::

::: {.column width="5%"}
:::

::: {.column width="30%" .phase-card .phase-day2}
#### Day 2
**Go deeper, work smarter**

- Debrief self-study experiences
- Examples: `planning` and using `#tools`, `skills`, `/agents`, and `/prompt-files`.
- Examples: customizing agents with `skills` and `/prompt-files`
- Other relevant techniques
:::

::::


## Logistics

| | Morning | Evening (repeat) |
|-------0--|----------------|----------------|
| **Format** | In person + virtual | Virtual only |
| **Session 1** | Mon Apr 20, 9 AM–12 PM ET | Mon Apr 20, 9 PM–12 AM ET |
| **Session 2 (repeat)** | Thu Apr 23, 9 AM–12 PM ET | Thu Apr 23, 9 PM–12 AM ET |

- Questions during session: **use Teams chat** or raise hand please

## Today's Agenda {.tiny-slide}

<!-- TODO: update with actual agenda after finalizing slides -->

[Part 1 — Setup & Live Demo (80 min)]{.fg}

| Time   | Topic |
|--------|--------------------------------------------------|
| 10 min | Welcome, introductions, course overview |
| 30 min | Software overview: Positron, GitHub Copilot, and Stata setup |
| 40 min | AI in action with Stata (R): my typical data workflow using AI |

[☕ Break (10 min)]{.fg}

[Part 2 — Concepts & Practice (90 min)]{.fg}

| Time   | Topic |
|--------|--------------------------------------------------|
| 30 min | AI overview presentation: how GitHub Copilot and LLMs work, key concepts, and capabilities |
| 30 min | Cookbook: securing sensitive data, guardrails, and responsible AI use |
| 30 min | Self-study exercises overview and Q&A |

## Course resources

- Slides and tutorials: [worldbank.github.io/ai4coding](https://worldbank.github.io/ai4coding).

- Code for exercises: [github.com/worldbank/ai4coding-examples](https://github.com/worldbank/ai4coding-examples).

- Data for exercises (OneDrive - WB only) [ ai4coding-data](https://worldbankgroup-my.sharepoint.com/:f:/g/personal/ebukin_worldbank_org/IgBy2yGjpLJPRbM67FJpBFVgAbMRYhEPmS30H2RJSBFO_gE?e=GlDEiR)

- Previous seminar on [AI-Assisted Coding in Stata and R](https://worldbank.github.io/geoPovLearn/seminars/seminar-2026-02/overview.html) on [GeoPovlearn](https://worldbank.github.io/geoPovLearn)
